brand.name: All Point Consultants
brand.vertical: B2B/Professional services — financial wellness & business formation consulting

summary: All Point Consultants is a Florida-based, founder-led consulting firm (Glenn Grahl) that helps individuals and small business owners untangle their personal and business finances through structured service tracks — a one-time Clarity assessment, ongoing Guided support, and a Business Readiness track for entity structuring, business credit, and funding. It serves people who feel overwhelmed by damaged or thin credit, intertwined personal/business finances, and unclear paths to funding, often after a triggering event like a loan denial or business launch. The firm leads with education over urgency, offering free guides and a free 15-minute assessment as the entry point to mid-to-high-ticket engagements.

campaigns.name: Lead-Magnet Nurture
campaigns.objective: Move PDF downloaders from curious to a booked free 15-minute assessment by framing the real problem behind their funding/credit question and building trust over a long, education-first cycle.
campaigns.trigger: Download of a gated PDF ('27 Killer Ways to Get Cash' or 'The Ultimate Business Credit & Financing Guide').
campaigns.segment: Leads below the sales-ready threshold; branch by which guide they downloaded (personal/cash-access vs. business-credit/funding).
campaigns.rationale: Both free PDFs are live and almost certainly generating downloads with zero automated follow-up — the single fastest revenue unlock for this brand, bridging cold downloaders to the free assessment that is the conversion event.

emails.purpose: Deliver the requested guide instantly and reframe the surface question ('how do I get cash/credit?') into the real problem All Point solves — how personal credit, business credit, and structure interconnect — then offer the free 15-minute assessment as the no-pressure next step.
emails.subject: Your '27 Killer Ways to Get Cash' guide is inside
emails.preview: Plus the one thing the guide can't tell you about your situation.
emails.cta: Download your guide → then book a free 15-minute assessment
keyPoints: Deliver the PDF immediately with a clear download button — this is the most-opened email they'll get from All Point
Reframe: cash and credit aren't separate problems — your personal credit, business structure, and funding readiness are one connected system, and tactics without that map backfire
Acknowledge the skepticism head-on: this is education, not a quick-fix credit-repair scheme — no obligations, no auto-enrollment
Soft CTA to the free 15-minute assessment via TidyCal, framed as 'see your own picture mapped,' in Glenn's calm advisor voice
Include standard financial-consulting disclaimer (not registered investment advice) and CAN-SPAM footer

campaigns.name: Assessment-to-Track Conversion
campaigns.objective: Convert a completed free 15-minute assessment into a paid Clarity, Guided, or Business Readiness track engagement.
campaigns.trigger: Free assessment completed (attended), with behavioral nudges for those who booked but stalled.
campaigns.segment: Leads who completed an assessment; branch by recommended track (one-time Clarity vs. ongoing Guided vs. Business Readiness).
campaigns.rationale: This is the core revenue play — the assessment is the 'aha,' and the window right after it is when the client can see their mapped picture and is most ready to commit to a structured path forward.

emails.purpose: Same-day recap that captures the clarity from the assessment in writing — their mapped financial picture and the specific prioritized next steps — then recommends the right track and makes committing low-friction.
emails.subject: Your financial picture — and the 3 steps we mapped together
emails.preview: Here's the written plan from our call, and where to go from here.
emails.cta: Review your recommended track and reserve your spot
keyPoints: Recap THEIR specific situation discussed on the call — how their personal credit, business structure, and funding readiness connect — so the value feels tangible, not generic
Restate the prioritized next steps in writing; the written plan IS the product they came for
Recommend the fitting track (Clarity / Guided / Business Readiness) and explain why it matches their stage, addressing cost uncertainty with a clear what-you-get framing
Disarm the 'I can figure this out myself' objection with the cost of guessing vs. a structured path; offer a reply-to-Glenn line for questions
Single primary CTA to start; calm and reassuring, never urgent or pressuring

campaigns.name: Assessment Booking, Reminders & No-Show Recovery
campaigns.objective: Maximize attended free assessments and recover the ones that slip, since the assessment is both the first-value moment and the gateway to every paid track.
campaigns.trigger: Assessment booked via TidyCal; upcoming appointment; no-show event.
campaigns.segment: Anyone with a scheduled free 15-minute assessment.
campaigns.rationale: The assessment is the 'aha' moment and the single conversion event for this brand — reminders cut no-shows by ~35%, and a value-add prep email raises both show rate and session quality so clients arrive ready to act.

emails.purpose: Day-before reminder that doubles as prep — confirms the time, tells them exactly what to bring and expect, and primes them to get maximum value so the session converts.
emails.subject: Your free assessment is tomorrow at [time] — here's how to prep
emails.preview: Bring these 3 things and we'll map your full picture in 15 minutes.
emails.cta: Confirm, add to calendar, or reschedule
keyPoints: Confirm date/time with add-to-calendar and a frictionless reschedule link to prevent silent no-shows
Set expectations: in 15 minutes Glenn will help map where your personal credit, business structure, and funding readiness stand — you'll leave with clear next steps
Short prep checklist (e.g., a rough sense of current credit situation, whether a business entity exists, your top financial goal) so the call is high-value
Reassure: it's a conversation, not a sales pitch — no obligation, no auto-enrollment
Personalize with the assessment type and Glenn's name; calm, welcoming founder voice

campaigns.name: Client Welcome & Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Get a new client through the secure portal intake and oriented to the 4-phase engagement (Discovery → Plan → Implementation → Review) so they reach first value and stay engaged between sessions.
campaigns.trigger: Paid track started / client portal account created.
campaigns.segment: New clients; branch by track (Clarity, Guided, or Business Readiness).
campaigns.rationale: New clients face long multi-phase cycles with unclear milestones — the top churn risk — so a structured welcome that anchors the process and drives the first portal action protects the engagement from day one.

emails.purpose: Instant warm welcome that sets expectations for the structured journey and drives the single most important first action — completing the portal intake — so the work can begin.
emails.subject: Welcome to All Point — your plan starts here
emails.preview: One quick step to set up your portal and kick off Discovery.
keyPoints: Warm, founder-led welcome from Glenn; thank them for trusting the process and reassure they're no longer guessing alone
Walk through the 4 phases (Discovery → Plan → Implementation → Review) so the road ahead feels clear and milestones are visible
Single first action: finish the secure portal intake so Glenn can prepare before the first working session
Set the cadence and 'hit reply anytime — a real person reads it' expectation to keep momentum between sessions
Calm, plain-language tone; financial-consulting disclaimer and CAN-SPAM footer included

campaigns.name: Value Newsletter
campaigns.objective: Stay top-of-mind and build authority across the long sales cycle with education-first content, keeping cold downloaders warm and existing clients engaged between sessions without discounting.
campaigns.trigger: Scheduled cadence (monthly to start, given unknown list health).
campaigns.segment: All engaged contacts; suppress active onboarding sequences and route long-dormant contacts to a separate win-back motion.
campaigns.rationale: All Point is an education-first brand with no visible newsletter and a large cold PDF-download list — a recurring value send is the always-on engine that builds the trust this trust-driven category runs on and re-warms leads toward an assessment.

emails.purpose: Flagship inaugural edition that teaches one genuinely useful idea — why mixing personal and business credit quietly blocks funding — establishing the recurring 'one clear idea' format and the brand's authority.
emails.subject: The wall between your personal and business credit (and why it's leaking)
emails.preview: A 3-minute read that explains what most owners learn too late.
emails.cta: Read the breakdown (or book a free assessment to map your own)
keyPoints: Lead with one clear, useful idea: how commingled personal and business credit silently caps your funding and raises your risk — explained with a plain-language analogy, no jargon
Give one concrete, do-it-yourself takeaway so the email earns its place even if they never buy (80% value / 20% soft offer)
Establish a recurring habit-building section (e.g., 'This month's clarity tip') so readers learn to expect value
Single soft CTA: read more, or book a free 15-minute assessment to map their own picture
Calm, authoritative advisor voice; financial-consulting disclaimer and one-click unsubscribe per CAN-SPAM
